breakdown: This PR widens state flag semantics in game_state.py to accept LLM numeric booleans (1, '1', 0, '0') alongside traditional boolean values. The changes are focused and solve a real production issue where LLM-generated state updates with numeric booleans were being silently rejected, causing level-up stalls. Type safety is excellent with explicit bool/int discrimination. Test coverage is comprehensive with clear subTest usage.
key_changes: Widen _is_state_flag_true to accept int(1) and string '1'
Widen _is_state_flag_false to accept int(0) and string '0'
Add isinstance check to prevent bool/int confusion (bool is subclass of int in Python)
Add comprehensive StateFlagSemanticsTest class with 5 test methods covering accepted/rejected values
Update docstrings to explain WHY (production LLM state update failures)
